title: Graph Theory, Networks, Scheduling, and Routing · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Design routes and schedules for deliveries and shared services. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a network routing and scheduling analysis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent vertices, edges, direction, weights, and attributes from a real system.
Analyze paths, cycles, components, reachability, and bottlenecks.
Use trees and spanning structures for hierarchy and low-cost connectivity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A model passes through (4, 14) and (8, 26). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Network Tradeoffs for a network routing and scheduling analysis.
steps.why: The Network Tradeoffs result for a network routing and scheduling analysis is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Network Tradeoffs, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a network routing and scheduling analysis. hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (26 − 14) / (8 − 4).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
